rationale: The citing paper focuses on logo asymmetry, brand personality, and arousal-driven congruence for brand equity, while the cited paper studies download-screen color effects on perceived waiting speed/relaxation in web contexts, so direct relevance is not obvious from topic alone. However, there is plausible conceptual overlap (visual design cue affecting affective state/perception), and the evidence here includes only a DOI-level reference match with no in-text citation context, so it is not safe to confidently drop.
